title: rhinanthus major subsp. bosniacus
intro: Rhinanthus major subsp. bosniacus, also known as Bosnian yellow rattle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family that grows in the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. Rhinanthus major subsp. bosniacus is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
